response.justification: The candidate has a broad 17-year software career and demonstrates strong agile practices, but the role's core requirement is sustained, production-grade C++ development with multi-threading, industrial networking, and IEC 62443 secure development lifecycle experience. The candidate's only C++ exposure is a single 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015; every subsequent position has been TypeScript, Node.js, or web-oriented. There is no evidence of industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), concurrent systems programming, or cybersecurity standards relevant to industrial automation.
strengths: Strong testing culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) practiced consistently across multiple roles
Event-driven and distributed systems exposure through GISCollective and Sociomantic high-throughput microservices work
Active knowledge-sharing via workshops, conference talks, and code kata sessions demonstrating mentoring capability
concerns: C++ exposure limited to one 6-month role a decade ago; no modern, sustained C++ or STL development
No demonstrated knowledge of IEC 62443, SDL, or any industrial cybersecurity practices
No background in industrial automation, embedded networking protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, DNP 3.0), or MQTT
